narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have them paused like this feels deeply unfair, and I'm truly sorry you're facing it. Our prediction model looked at your profile and saw an 87% chance of struggling to pass the bar exam down the line, painting a picture of a tough road ahead based on patterns from thousands of similar journeys. What stood out most was your Year 1 decile of 1.0, the strongest signal pulling toward this outcome, suggesting an early struggle with the rigors of law school coursework that often sets the tone for what's to come. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 29.0, which the model saw as another big hurdle, as it reflects the kind of analytical foundation that bar success tends to hinge on. Your Year 3 decile of 1.0 reinforced this, showing that even later on, the academic climb looked steeper than for peers who powered through higher. Layered in was your middle family income bracket, which in the model's view, might limit access to the extra tutoring or support that sometimes bridges these gaps. Your background as a Black female student also factored in as tilting toward challenges, drawing from historical trends where systemic barriers have made the path harder. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.9 added to that sense of uneven momentum coming in, while being full-time didn't shift the tide enough. Together, these pieces wove a story of resilience tested against steep odds, not a judgment on your potential, but a cautious flag from data that's seen too many bright sparks flicker out at the bar. You've got fire in you—maybe bolstering that LSAT prep or seeking mentorship could rewrite this script next time. Hang in there; this isn't the end of your story.
